elements.text: 5605.8: Cost and income approaches, and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: reconciliation of market value (Future effective date
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cost approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciliation of market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must include any approach to value that is necessary for credible results,
elements.label: text

elements.text: even if the Seller did not request it. The Seller may request the appraiser to develop and report
elements.label: text

elements.text: the cost approach or the income approach, even if not required for the transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Cost approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cost approach is required for appraisals of Manufactured Homes, but no other property
elements.label: text

elements.text: type. (See Section 5703.9(e) for Manufactured Homes requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: In Market Areas with unique property styles, a lack of comparable sales, or the presence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: unique features (such as outbuildings), the cost approach can provide support for adjustments
elements.label: text

elements.text: made in the sales comparison approach.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cost approach may be appropriate when appraising properties that are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New or proposed construction (i.e., the construction has not started and the description of
elements.label: text

elements.text: construction to be completed is based on plans and specifications and/or similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Under renovation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unique because of property features (e.g., outbuildings, stables, pole-barns, shops)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unique because of their styles or construction methods (e.g., barn conversions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“barndominiums”), “shouses” (living-space and work/storage combinations), berm
elements.label: text

elements.text: homes, log homes, or geodesic dome dwellings), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not typical for the Market Area or have functional obsolescence
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal reports that rely solely on the cost approach for the opinion of market value are
elements.label: text

elements.text: unacceptable, except for appraisals completed for a HeritageOne® Mortgage. For special
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal requirements related HeritageOne Mortgages, see Section 4504.9.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not require an estimate of remaining economic life.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Income approach
elements.label: text

elements.text: The income approach is required for appraisals of 2- to 4-unit properties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisals that rely solely on the income approach for the opinion of market value are
elements.label: text

elements.text: unacceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reconciliation of market value
elements.label: text

elements.text: The data, valuation approaches and information presented in the appraisal report must justify
elements.label: text

elements.text: and support the appraiser’s opinion of market value. The appraisal report must contain an
elements.label: text

elements.text: explanation of how the final value conclusion was determined, and the rationale must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: consistent with the comments, conclusions and assumptions stated throughout the appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The reconciliation must contain any conditions of the appraisal on which the final opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value is based.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the subject transaction involves sales or financing concessions, the appraiser’s opinion of
elements.label: text

elements.text: market value must reflect the value of the subject property without the concessions.
elements.label: text

elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains appraisal requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements and guidelines apply to completing Form 72, Small Residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: Income Property Appraisal Report, for 2- to 4-unit properties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Comparable rent data for 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least three comparable rentals must be analyzed in the “comparable rental data”
elements.label: text

elements.text: section. The comparable rentals must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have current rental information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be units similar to, and located near, the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report should state that the units and properties selected as comparable
elements.label: text

elements.text: rentals are comparable to the subject property (both the units and the overall property)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and should accurately represent the rental market for the subject property unless
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise stated in the report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Rent schedule for 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The rent schedule section contains the subject property’s current actual rents and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: estimated market rents. The estimated market rents for the subject property must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: supported in the appraisal report and must be consistent with the data presented
elements.label: text

elements.text: throughout the report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Sales comparison approach for 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraisal report must contain the unadjusted units of comparison for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: comparable sale.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The units of comparison are:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales price per square foot of gross building area
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales price per unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sales price per room
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Gross rent multiplier
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transactions, the appraisal report must also contain the units of comparison
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the comment area of the sales comparison analysis, the appraisal report must include a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation of the adjusted sales prices and the unadjusted units of comparison, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate, in the same way properties are sold in the Market Area. The appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: must include commentary that describes the factors that are deemed most relevant by
elements.label: text

elements.text: typical investors or purchasers in the Market Area when purchasing a similar property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5701.8(a) for additional appraisal requirements for units in Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5703.9 for additional appraisal requirements for Manufactured Homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5704.2 for additional appraisal requirements for leasehold estates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Chapter 5705 for additional appraisal requirements for Cooperative Units.

elements.text: 5605.10: Special property appraisal requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (Future effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements and guidelines apply to completing a Traditional Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report for 2- to 4-unit properties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Comparable rent data for 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least three comparable rentals must be analyzed in the “comparable rental analysis”
elements.label: text

elements.text: section. The comparable rentals must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have current rental information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be units similar to the subject property
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Rent schedule for 2- to 4-unit properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: The rent schedule section contains the subject property’s current actual rents and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: estimated market rents. The estimated market rents for the subject property must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: supported in the appraisal report and must be consistent with the data presented
elements.label: text

elements.text: throughout the report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5701.8(a) for additional appraisal requirements for units in Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5703.9 for additional appraisal requirements for Manufactured Homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Leasehold estates
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 5704.2 for additional appraisal requirements for leasehold estates.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Cooperative Share Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Chapter 5705 for additional appraisal requirements for Cooperative Units.

elements.text: 5606.1: Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) (01/26/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information and requirements related to the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of UAD
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) UAD
elements.label: text

elements.text: The UAD standardizes key appraisal data elements for a subset of fields on certain uniform
elements.label: text

elements.text: residential appraisal report forms and includes all data points required to complete the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report forms.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The UAD Specification documents the business and technical requirements for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementation of the UAD. The UAD Specification, which may be amended from time to
elements.label: text

elements.text: time, can be found on www.freddiemac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix D provides field-specific standardization requirements for completing Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s residential appraisal report forms that are required to be completed using the UAD.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix D lists the requirements for the data that must be included in specific forms and
elements.label: text

elements.text: how the data should be reported on the appraisal report form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Use of UAD
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following appraisal report forms must be completed using the UAD in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix D when reporting results of an appraisal:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 70, Uniform Residential Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 70D, Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Desktop)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 70H, Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Hybrid)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 465, Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 465H, Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report (Hybrid)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 466, Exterior-Only Inspection Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 2055, Exterior-Only Inspection Residential Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Other appraisal report forms may be completed using the standards contained in the UAD
elements.label: text

elements.text: Specification to the extent those standards are applicable to that particular form.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5606.1: Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD) (Future
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: effective date 11/02/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information and requirements related to the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Appraisal Dataset (UAD)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use of UAD
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) UAD
elements.label: text

elements.text: The UAD standardizes key appraisal data elements for the Uniform Residential Appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report (URAR), Restricted Appraisal Update Reports and Completion Reports and includes
elements.label: text

elements.text: all data points required to complete the reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The UAD Specification documents the business and technical requirements for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementation of the UAD. The UAD Specification, which may be amended from time to
elements.label: text

elements.text: time, can be found on www.freddiemac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix F-1 provides field-specific standardization requirements for completing Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s residential appraisal reports that are required to be completed using the UAD.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix F-1 lists the requirements for the data that must be included for specific property
elements.label: text

elements.text: types and how the data should be reported on the appraisal report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix F-2 provides field-specific standardization requirements for completing Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s Restricted Appraisal Update Reports that are required to be completed using the UAD.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix F-2 lists the requirements for the data that must be included and how the data
elements.label: text

elements.text: should be reported on the appraisal update report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appendix F-3 provides field-specific standardization requirements for completing Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s Completion Reports that are required to be completed using the UAD. Appendix F-3
elements.label: text

elements.text: lists the requirements for the data that must be included and how the data should be reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: on the completion report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Use of UAD
elements.label: text

elements.text: Appraisal reports must be completed using the UAD in accordance with Appendix F-1 when
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting results of an appraisal, Appendix F-2 when reporting the results of an appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: update and Appendix F-3 when reporting results of a completion report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following appraisal report review forms may be completed using the standards contained
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the UAD Specification to the extent those standards are applicable to that particular
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information and requirements related to the:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Uniform Collateral Data Portal® (UCDP®)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery of appraisals through the UCDP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ UCDP messaging
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data submitted to the UCDP
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) UCDP
elements.label: text

elements.text: The UCDP is a portal for the electronic collection and delivery of certain appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: forms and the associated appraisal data to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Delivery of appraisals through the UCDP
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages that require appraisal reports, the following appraisal report forms, including
elements.label: text

elements.text: all exhibits and addenda, must be submitted to the UCDP and receive a “Successful” status
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Delivery Date of the Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 70, Uniform Residential Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 70B, Manufactured Home Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 70D, Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Desktop)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 70H, Uniform Residential Appraisal Report (Hybrid)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 72, Small Residential Income Property Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 465, Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 465H, Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report (Hybrid)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 466, Exterior-Only Inspection Individual Condominium Unit Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 2055, Exterior-Only Inspection Residential Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fannie Mae Form 2090, Individual Cooperative Interest Appraisal Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers have the option of submitting Form 442, Appraisal Update and/or Completion Report,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the UCDP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac appraisal report forms not listed above must not be submitted to the UCDP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may use the UCDP as a tool to aid in determining UAD compliance. However, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: submission of appraisal report forms to the UCDP does not relieve the Seller from Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac appraisal requirements, including the requirement that the appraisal report must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Appendix D of the UAD, and does not affect Seller representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding appraisals and the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller’s ability to select an appraisal management company or other party in connection
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the use of the UCDP does not constitute Freddie Mac’s endorsement or approval of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal management company or other party and does not relieve Seller of any obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the Guide or Seller’s other Purchase Documents, including the requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 3101.1 related to the Freddie Mac Exclusionary List.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) UCDP messaging
elements.label: text

elements.text: When an appraisal report is submitted to the UCDP, the Seller may receive a variety of
elements.label: text

elements.text: feedback messages designed to assist the Seller in evaluating the appraisal report to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine whether it meets the requirements of this topic (e.g., the accuracy of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraiser’s opinion of market value, UAD compliance, etc.). The Seller should be prepared
elements.label: text

elements.text: to address these feedback messages as part of their appraisal report review and property
elements.label: text

elements.text: underwriting process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For certain Mortgages, the Seller may receive a message indicating the appraisal report is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for collateral representation and warranty relief. See Section 5602.2 for more details
elements.label: text

elements.text: and eligibility requirements related to this representation and warranty relief.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The presence of one or more feedback message(s) with a “warning” severity indicator
elements.label: text

elements.text: does not prevent the “successful” submission of an appraisal report to the UCDP and does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not deem the property ineligible or the appraisal report unacceptable. In addition, the absence
elements.label: text

elements.text: of feedback messages does not represent Freddie Mac’s acceptance of the appraised value or
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief from Seller representations and warranties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Data submitted to the UCDP
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and its agents and contractors have the right to use, reproduce, modify, disclose,
elements.label: text

elements.text: sublicense, distribute and retain all information and data submitted to the UCDP for delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: to Freddie Mac. This includes any fields on an appraisal report and the contents thereof, all
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and data entered by the Seller or on the Seller’s behalf and any other information
elements.label: text

elements.text: and data obtained by or transmitted through the UCDP (collectively, the “UCDP Data”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may use this data for any purpose related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The UCDP, the appraisal report, the loan and any securities related to the UCDP Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Analytics, modeling, quality control, fraud detection, information security and similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Data and services obtained or provided by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Internal functions, including, without limitation, system monitoring, maintenance and
elements.label: text

elements.text: security
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Compliance with applicable laws, regulations, court orders and the order of an agency
elements.label: text

elements.text: that either regulates or has jurisdiction over Freddie Mac; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Enforcement of Freddie Mac’s rights and remedies

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2025-7, which announced the policy requirements for Uniform Appraisal

elements.text: Dataset (UAD) 3.6. Sellers may submit to the Uniform Collateral Data Portal® appraisal
elements.label: text

elements.text: reports that use UAD 3.6 before the mandatory effective November 2, 2026 version of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic appraisal report requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unacceptable electronic appraisal report practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller liabilities and responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Electronic appraisal report requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller may use and maintain an Electronic Record of the appraisal report as an original
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage file document if the following conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The electronic appraisal report otherwise complies with the applicable appraisal report
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Topic 5600
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The appraiser electronically transmits the electronic appraisal report directly to the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: or a third party authorized by the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The electronically transmitted photographs and any addenda are clear and meet the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for exhibits and addenda outlined in Section 5604.2
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Seller represents and warrants that the appraiser’s Electronic Signature, and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: supervisory appraiser’s signature if applicable, are attached to, or logically associated

elements.text: 5701.1: Seller’s assessment of Condominium Project
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: risks; terms used in this chapter (05/06/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller’s assessment of Condominium Project risks
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Glossary definitions and other terms used in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Seller’s assessment of Condominium Project risks
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requires a Condominium Project review to address certain project risks
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The marketability and condition of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The marketability of the units within the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The financial stability and viability of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project-level litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Restrictions on unit owners’ rights to occupy the unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ownership and use of the Common Elements and Amenities; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The adequacy of insurance coverage to protect the project from damage and loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac expects the Seller to have staff that is experienced and knowledgeable about
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project risks and to place as much emphasis on the adequacy of the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: as collateral as it does on underwriting the Borrower’s creditworthiness. The quality of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage secured by a unit in a Condominium Project can be impacted by the financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: stability and viability of the particular project, among other project characteristics.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The conclusion that a Mortgage is acceptable to Freddie Mac must be based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination that the Borrower is creditworthy and the Mortgaged Premises is adequate
elements.label: text

elements.text: collateral for the Mortgage transaction.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller determines that an Established Condominium Project does not meet certain
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac project eligibility requirements and concludes that the Mortgaged Premises is
elements.label: text

elements.text: still adequate collateral for the Mortgage transaction, then the Seller may request that Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac consider a waiver. For information on requesting Freddie Mac to consider a waiver of
elements.label: text

elements.text: its project eligibility requirements, see Section 5701.11.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: to, sea walls, elevators, waterproofing, stairwells, balconies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is:

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accomplished within the project’s normal operating budget

elements.text: or through special assessments that are within guidelines

elements.text: secured by units in any of the following types of projects are not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Condominium projects in which the unit owners do not have an undivided ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest or leasehold interest in the land on which the project is located
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project in which, when control of the Homeowners Association (HOA) has been or will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: turned over to the unit owners, the unit owners do not have either: (1) an undivided
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership interest in the land on which the project is located; or (2) a leasehold interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: land on which the project is located.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Condominium Hotel or similar type of transient housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any project that is a Condominium Hotel or similar type of transient housing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Condominium Hotel characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects with one or more of the following characteristics are considered a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Hotel and are ineligible projects:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Projects and/or HOAs that are licensed, have a permit to operate, or are registered, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: a hotel or a motel, even though the units may be individually owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Projects that impose mandatory rental-pooling (or similar agreements that restrict the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit owner’s ability to occupy the unit for living purposes such as blackout dates or
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupancy limits on personal use) to assure an inventory of units for rent on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: frequent basis, such as daily, weekly, monthly or seasonally
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Projects with revenue-sharing agreements between unit owners and the HOA,
elements.label: text

elements.text: property management and/or rental operator contracted by the HOA or property
elements.label: text

elements.text: management
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Transient housing characteristics
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects with one or more of the following characteristics are considered a type of
elements.label: text

elements.text: transient housing and are ineligible projects:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Projects and/or HOAs that are licensed (or have a designated licensed agent), have a
elements.label: text

elements.text: permit to operate, or are registered, as a type of transient housing (e.g., vacation rental
elements.label: text

elements.text: license, short term rental registrant, etc.) for the rental of non-HOA owned units
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Condominium Projects that are conversions of a hotel (or a conversion of a similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: type of transient housing) unless the project was a Gut Rehabilitation and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: resulting Condominium Units no longer have the characteristics of a hotel or similar
elements.label: text

elements.text: type of transient housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The HOA receives revenue from, or pays expenses for, hotel type services including
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Registration desk services. This includes a project’s front desk staff also serving
elements.label: text

elements.text: as a vacation rentals employee or assisting with an onsite rental operator’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: registration desk or any payments received from renting units on a transient basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: via the rental registration desk. It does not include any market rate rent paid by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: third party for the use of a registration desk.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A rental registration website/hosting platform
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The HOA charges a fee, paid by either the unit owner or the unit owner’s transient
elements.label: text

elements.text: renters, when a unit is rented on a transient basis. This includes any surcharge to unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: owners who do not elect to rent their units through the HOA’s and/or property
elements.label: text

elements.text: management’s preferred rental operator(s). This does not include any fees charged to
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimburse the cost of the wear and tear to the project’s facilities and/or Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the transient renters or any fees charged for reviewing the terms of the transient
elements.label: text

elements.text: rental contract.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The HOA and/or its management agent, as rental operator or licensed agent, collects
elements.label: text

elements.text: and remits required taxes to all applicable jurisdictions (city, county and State) such
elements.label: text

elements.text: as transient, short-term rental, and/or hotel occupancy taxes as well as sales taxes,
elements.label: text

elements.text: excise taxes, etc.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The HOA provides a designated space (e.g., an HOA-owned unit, an area in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project’s lobby or other Common Elements area, etc.) for the operation of an on-site
elements.label: text

elements.text: rental operator free of charge to that rental operator
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Unit owners are required through the Project Documents or other contractual
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement to use a specific rental agency(ies) for their transient rentals
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. The entity that manages the Condominium Project also manages its transient rentals.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This does not include an entity that has independent divisions for property
elements.label: text

elements.text: management and for transient rentals management, the property management division
elements.label: text

elements.text: manages the Condominium Project, and unit owners are not mandated to contract
elements.label: text

elements.text: with its transient rentals division.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Residential units have restrictions on interior decorating that are imposed by the HOA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or its management agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Examples of personalized services and centralized systems that are common red
elements.label: text

elements.text: flags of a Condominium Hotel or transient housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are examples of personalized services and centralized systems that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: common red flags of a Condominium Hotel or similar type of transient housing:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Personalized services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Daily cleaning services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Porters/luggage service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Room service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Centralized systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any central telephone service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Central key systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects with short-term rentals, personalized services and/or centralized
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems may be eligible if the Seller fully analyzes all the characteristics of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: and related information to determine if the project is not a Condominium Hotel or
elements.label: text

elements.text: transient housing as described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must retain, and provide upon request, documentation to support its analysis that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Condominium Project is not a Condominium Hotel or similar type of transient housing as
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in Section 5701.2(b)(6). Such documentation may include, but is not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project Documents (e.g., by-laws, project budgets and financial statements), offering
elements.label: text

elements.text: statements (or their equivalent) and marketing materials, websites, contracts for sale and
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Condominium Project with multi-dwelling units
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project in which an owner may hold a single deed evidencing ownership of more than one
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling unit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Condominium Project with excessive commercial or non-residential space
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project in which more than 35% of the total above and below grade square footage of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project (or more than 35% of the total above and below grade square footage of the building
elements.label: text

elements.text: in which the project is located) is used as commercial or non-residential space.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In calculating the amount of commercial or non-residential space, Sellers must determine:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The total square footage of the project (or the building in which the project is located);
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The square footage of the commercial or non-residential space; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The residential space square footage
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller will then divide the total commercial or non-residential square footage by the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: square footage of the project or building to determine the total amount of commercial or non￾residential space. Below is a table illustrating what must be included or may be excluded
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the calculation of commercial or non-residential space:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Tenancy-in-common apartment project
elements.label: text

elements.text: A tenancy-in-common apartment project is owned by several owners as tenants-in-common
elements.label: text

elements.text: or by an HOA. Individuals have an undivided interest in the residential apartment building
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including the units) and land on which the building is located and may or may not have the
elements.label: text

elements.text: right of exclusive occupancy of a specific apartment unit in the building.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Timeshare project or project with segmented ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project in which there is an arrangement under which a purchaser receives an interest in
elements.label: text

elements.text: real estate and the right to use a unit or Amenities, or both, for a specified period and on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recurring basis such as the 15th week of the year, or ownership that is for a limited period
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as for the subsequent five years.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Houseboat project
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project comprised of boats that have been designed or modified to be used primarily as
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwelling units.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Condominium Project in which the unit owners do not possess sole ownership of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as stated below, unit owners in a Condominium Project must have the sole ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: in and the right to the use of the Common Elements, including all buildings, roads, parking,
elements.label: text

elements.text: facilities and Amenities. The developer must not retain any ownership interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements, facilities and Amenities, except as unit owner.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Condominium Project that shares Amenities with one or more other residential projects is
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible if the projects share the Amenities (such as recreational or fitness facilities,
elements.label: text

elements.text: swimming pools and clubhouses) for the sole use of the unit owners and Shareholders, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable. The term “residential projects” includes only residential Condominium Projects,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cooperative Projects and Planned Unit Developments (PUDs). The residential projects must
elements.label: text

elements.text: have an agreement specifying:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. A description of the shared Amenities and the terms of unit owners’ and Shareholders’
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted use of the shared Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. How the shared Amenities will be funded, managed and maintained, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The method for resolving disputes between the projects regarding the shared Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Common Elements, including Amenities, such as parking and recreational facilities,
elements.label: text

elements.text: must not be subject to a lease between the unit owners or the HOA (as lessee) and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: party (as lessor).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Parking provided under commercial leases or permit arrangements for parking, entered into
elements.label: text

elements.text: with parties unrelated to the developer are acceptable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Condominium Project in litigation
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project in which: (i) the HOA is named as a party to pending litigation or the Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: discovers that the HOA is a party to an Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) proceeding,
elements.label: text

elements.text: such as arbitration or mediation, or (ii) the project sponsor or developer is named as a party
elements.label: text

elements.text: to pending litigation, or the Seller discovers that the project sponsor or developer is a party in
elements.label: text

elements.text: an ADR proceeding and, in either case, the dispute relates to the safety, structural soundness,
elements.label: text

elements.text: functional use or habitability of the project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller determines that the pending litigation or ADR proceeding involves only minor
elements.label: text

elements.text: matters that do not affect the safety, structural soundness, functional use or habitability of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project, the project is eligible if the litigation or ADR proceeding is limited to one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The litigation amount is known, the insurance company has committed to provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: defense and the litigation amount is covered by the insurance policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The litigation amount is unknown, the Seller has documented the Mortgage file with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the complaint, or the most recent amended complaint, and with an attorney letter
elements.label: text

elements.text: that supports the Seller’s determination that the litigation involves minor matters. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: attorney letter must state all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. The reason for the litigation;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. That the insurance company has committed to provide the defense; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. That any potential monetary judgment against the HOA, or settlement with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HOA, including punitive damages, will likely be covered by the HOA’s insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the attorney indicates the matter will not likely be covered by the HOA’s insurance
elements.label: text

elements.text: policy, then the project is ineligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The matter involves any of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. A non-monetary neighbor dispute or right of quiet enjoyment, whether litigated or
elements.label: text

elements.text: in an ADR proceeding;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. A dispute in which the HOA is the plaintiff in a foreclosure action or action for
elements.label: text

elements.text: past due HOA assessments; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. A dispute in which the HOA is the plaintiff in the litigation or a party to an ADR
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeding and is seeking reimbursement for expenditures made to repair the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project’s component(s). The expenditures may have included items that related to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the safety, structural soundness, functional use or habitability of the project,
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that the repair permanently resolved the defect or issue, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenditures did not significantly impact the financial stability or future solvency
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the HOA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The estimated or known amount in dispute in the litigation or ADR proceeding is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: expected to exceed 10% of the project’s funded reserves, provided that use of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: project’s funded reserves to pay for project litigation or dispute resolution does not
elements.label: text

elements.text: violate the applicable jurisdiction’s laws and regulations
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must retain documentation to support its analysis that the reason for the dispute
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets Freddie Mac’s requirements for minor matters as described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Condominium Project with excessive single investor concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any project in which an individual or a single entity such as an investor group, partnership or
elements.label: text

elements.text: corporation owns more than the following total number of units in the project:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excessive single investor concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Number of units in the project Total number of units owned by
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual or single entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Five- to 10-unit projects part of a Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Association
elements.label: text

elements.text: Two
elements.label: text

elements.text: Excessive single investor concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Number of units in the project Total number of units owned by
elements.label: text

elements.text: individual or single entity
elements.label: text

elements.text: 21 or more 25%
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Exclusion from calculation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following may be excluded from the single investor concentration calculation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Vacant units being actively marketed by the developer. Any units leased by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: developer must be included in the calculation of the developer’s percentage of
elements.label: text

elements.text: ownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Units that a non-profit entity controls or owns for the purpose of providing
elements.label: text

elements.text: affordable housing
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Units held in affordable housing programs (including units subject to non-eviction
elements.label: text

elements.text: rent regulation codes), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. Units retained for workforce housing by higher-education institutions
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Exception for a project with excessive single investor concentration:
elements.label: text

elements.text: For purchase transactions, a project with single investor concentration greater than
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified above will be eligible provided all of the following are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. The purchase transaction will result in a reduction of the single investor
elements.label: text

elements.text: concentration
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. The single investor must not own more than 49% of the units in the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. The Seller obtains evidence that the single investor is marketing units for sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the goal to decrease the single investor concentration to 25% or less of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: units in the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. The single investor is current on all HOA assessments, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: v. There are no planned or current special assessments in the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Continuing Care Retirement Community (CCRC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: A CCRC is a residential project specifically designed to meet the evolving health and
elements.label: text

elements.text: housing needs of seniors. Unlike age-restricted communities, residents in CCRCs sign a
elements.label: text

elements.text: contract in advance for a lifetime commitment of care from the facility, regardless of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: future health or housing needs. CCRCs may also be known as Life-Care Facilities.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Manufactured Homes in Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects that contain Manufactured Homes are ineligible unless they meet one
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the following exceptions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ They comply with the Established Condominium Project requirements in Section 5701.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Section 5701.5(e) for additional information specific to Manufactured Homes), or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ They have an “Approved by Fannie Mae” status designation in Fannie Mae’s Condo
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project ManagerTM (refer to Section 5701.9(a) for additional information on reciprocal
elements.label: text

elements.text: project reviews)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m)Condominium Project with mandatory dues or similar membership fees for use of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amenities such as clubhouses or recreational facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects with mandatory dues or similar membership fees, including initiation or joining fees,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which allow for the use of Amenities such as clubhouses or recreational facilities are
elements.label: text

elements.text: ineligible unless both of the following are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The HOA and/or Master Association solely own the Amenities; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Unit owners within the HOA or Master Association are the only persons
elements.label: text

elements.text: or entities eligible for membership, with full rights and privileges to the use of these
elements.label: text

elements.text: Amenities being the primary benefit of membership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (n) Project in need of Critical Repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by units in Condominium Projects in need of Critical Repairs, as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Glossary, are not eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For both current and planned (i.e., unit owners approved but the board has not initiated
elements.label: text

elements.text: collection yet) special assessments, Seller must obtain and review the following information
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each special assessment to determine if the funds are for a Critical Repair:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The purpose of the special assessment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the special assessment was approved,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The original amount of the special assessment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amount remaining to be collected, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The expected date the special assessment will be paid in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a structural and/or mechanical inspection has been completed within three years of Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: project review date, Seller must review that inspection report. There must not be any Critical
elements.label: text

elements.text: Repairs needed, as well as no current evacuation orders or similar regulatory actions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects in need of Critical Repairs remain ineligible until the required repairs and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection report have been completed and documented. Sellers must review an engineer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: report or substantially similar document to determine that the repairs resolved the building’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: safety, soundness, structural integrity or habitability concerns.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If damage or deferred maintenance is isolated to one or a few units and does not affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall safety, soundness, structural integrity, or habitability of the project, then this project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirement does not apply.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This requirement does not apply to Routine Repairs, as defined in the Glossary.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sellers may need to review a combination of documents to determine a project meets our
elements.label: text

elements.text: physical condition requirements. Some examples include but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ HOA board meeting minutes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Engineer’s reports,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Structural and/or mechanical inspection reports,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reserve studies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ List of necessary repairs provided by the HOA or management company,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ List of special assessments provided by the HOA or management company, and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other substantially similar documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: This list is not prescriptive or exhaustive. Sellers are responsible for determining which
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents they need to review to ensure compliance with this requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (o) Project with an evacuation order
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project with an evacuation order due to an unsafe condition, either for a partial or total
elements.label: text

elements.text: evacuation of the project’s building(s), is ineligible until the unsafe condition has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: remediated and the building(s) is safe for occupancy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (p) Projects terminating or involved in insolvency proceedings
elements.label: text

elements.text: A project must not be the subject of any action that would cause the project to cease to exist,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including termination, deconversion or dissolution of the project’s legal structure. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition, a project must not be the subject of a voluntary or involuntary bankruptcy,
elements.label: text

elements.text: insolvency, liquidation or receivership proceeding or any substantially similar action under
elements.label: text

elements.text: State or federal law. This includes a project that has voted or is in the process of voting on
elements.label: text

elements.text: any of the actions or proceedings described above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.4: Streamlined reviews (03/05/25)

elements.text: indicated in the following table:

elements.text: 5701.5: Established Condominium Projects (05/06/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project completion requirements for Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project budget requirements for Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquent assessments for Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements when a Seller relies on a project reserve study for Established Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements when an Established Condominium Project contains Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for the Established Condominium Projects review type, the Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: must meet the definition of an Established Condominium Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Condominium Unit Mortgages or the Condominium Projects do not comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements for (i) Condominium Projects with a Project Certified status Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Assessment Request (PAR) finding (see Section 5701.10) or (ii) streamlined reviews (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 5701.4), the Mortgages must comply with all of the following eligibility requirements in
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition to the project review and eligibility requirements in Section 5701.2.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Project completion requirements for Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: All units, Common Elements and Amenities must be complete.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Project budget requirements for Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project’s budget for the current fiscal year must comply with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Be consistent with the nature of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Appropriate assessments must be established to manage the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. There must be appropriate allocations for line items pertinent to the type and status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. There must be adequate funding for insurance deductible amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. At least 10% of the budget must provide funding for replacement reserves for capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenditures and deferred maintenance based on the project’s age, estimated remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: life and replacement cost of major Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculation of replacement reserve percentage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Formula Exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The replacement reserve percentage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined by dividing: (i) the annual
elements.label: text

elements.text: budgeted replacement reserve allocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: by (ii) the Homeowners Association’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: (HOA’s) annual budgeted assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: income (including regular common
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense fees)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The calculation may exclude:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Special assessment income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Income allocated to or in reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Incidental income not relied upon for
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance operations or capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. Amounts collected from unit owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: (but usually paid individually by them)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for items or utilities such as internet
elements.label: text

elements.text: access
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. A Seller may rely on a reserve study instead of the project budget providing a
elements.label: text

elements.text: replacement reserve of at least 10%, provided the conditions in section (d) below are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. An HOA must not receive more than 10% of its budgeted income from the rental or
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasing of commercial parking facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Delinquent assessments for Established Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) HOA assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of their HOA assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of each special assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Requirements when a Seller relies on a project reserve study for Established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The reserve study must comply with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The reserve study generally must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An inventory of major components of the project;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financial analysis and evaluation of current reserve fund adequacy; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proposed annual reserve funding plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A reserve study’s financial analysis must validate that the project has appropriately
elements.label: text

elements.text: allocated the recommended reserve funds to provide the Condominium Project with
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient financial protection comparable to Freddie Mac’s standard budget requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for replacement reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The reserve study’s annual reserve funding plan, which details total costs identified for
elements.label: text

elements.text: replacement components, must meet or exceed the study’s recommendation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The most current reserve study (or update) must be dated within 36 months of the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination that a Condominium Project is eligible (see Section 5701.2(a)(3))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The reserve study must be prepared by an independent expert skilled in performing such
elements.label: text

elements.text: studies (such as a reserve study professional, a construction engineer, a certified public
elements.label: text

elements.text: accountant who specializes in reserve studies or any professional with demonstrated
elements.label: text

elements.text: experience and knowledge in completing reserve studies)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The reserve study must meet or exceed requirements set forth in any applicable state
elements.label: text

elements.text: statutes
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. The reserve study must comment favorably on the project’s age, estimated remaining life,
elements.label: text

elements.text: structural integrity and the replacement of major components
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller relies on a reserve study that meets the requirements of this section, the project’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: budget must contain appropriate allocations to support the costs identified in the study.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain and retain in the Mortgage file a copy of the reserve study. The Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must also perform an analysis of the study and retain this analysis in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Requirements when an Established Condominium Project contains Manufactured
elements.label: text

elements.text: Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Mortgage must be secured by a Detached Condominium Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The subject Manufactured Home must not be a single-wide Manufactured Home
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The subject Manufactured Home must meet the requirements in Chapter 5703
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Although Section 5701.3(a) states otherwise, the Condominium Project’s unit owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: must only have an undivided ownership interest in the land on which the project is
elements.label: text

elements.text: located
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Condominium Project must not have campgrounds or other facilities for transient or
elements.label: text

elements.text: mobile units
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Condominium Unit Mortgages secured by Condominium Units located in Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects that contain Manufactured Homes that do not comply with these requirements, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: projects must have an “Approved by Fannie Mae” status designation in Fannie Mae’s Condo
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project ManagerTM (see Section 5701.9(a) for additional information) for the Mortgages to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligible for sale to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the requirements for Established Condominium Projects in this Section 5701.5 are met,
elements.label: text

elements.text: then the Seller is not required to comply with the requirements for any of the other project
elements.label: text

elements.text: review types in Sections 5701.4 (Streamlined reviews), 5701.6 (New Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: reviews), 5701.7 (Exempt from review) and 5701.9 (Reciprocal project reviews).
