elements.text: 5701.6: New Condominium Projects (05/06/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project completion requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Owner-occupancy requirements for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project budget requirement for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquent assessments for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Limitations on ability to sell/right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conversions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgagee consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rights of Condominium mortgagees and guarantors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ First mortgagee’s rights confirmed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements when a Seller relies on a project reserve study for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements when a Seller relies on contributions to a working capital fund for New
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New Condominium Project sold with excessive Seller contributions
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for the New Condominium Projects review type, the Condominium Project must
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet the definition of a New Condominium Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the project review and eligibility requirements in Section 5701.2, Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by Condominium Units in New Condominium Projects must comply with all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Project completion requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The subject legal phase (or the subject building) and any prior legal phases in which units
elements.label: text

elements.text: have been offered for sale must be substantially complete. “Substantially complete” indicates
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Common Elements are complete and the units are complete subject to the selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: of buyer preference items.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purpose of determining project completion under this Section 5701.6, a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: building can only have one legal phase, regardless of whether the Condominium Project is
elements.label: text

elements.text: comprised solely of that single building or multiple buildings. Legal phases are defined by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Project Documents. Construction or marketing phases developed for the convenience of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the developer are not necessarily legal phases and are not eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Owner-occupancy requirements for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least 50% of the total units in the project (or at least 50% of the sum of the subject legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: phase and prior legal phases) must have been conveyed or must be under contract to
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchasers who will occupy the units as their Primary Residences or second homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purpose of calculating owner-occupancy under this Section 5701.6, a single building
elements.label: text

elements.text: can only have one legal phase, regardless of whether the Condominium Project is comprised
elements.label: text

elements.text: solely of that single building or multiple buildings. Legal phases are defined by the Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents. Construction or marketing phases developed for the convenience of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: developer are not necessarily legal phases and are not eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Project budget requirement for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project’s budget for the current fiscal year must comply with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Homeowners Association’s (HOA’s) assessments must begin once the developer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: ceased to pay operating expenses attributable to the Condominium Project, whether or
elements.label: text

elements.text: not all units have been sold. When any unit owner other than the developer pays
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments, the developer must pay the assessments attributable to the unsold units.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The project’s budget (or its projected budget if the project has not been turned over to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit owners) must be consistent with the nature of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Appropriate assessments must be established to manage the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. There must be appropriate allocations for line items pertinent to the type and status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the project was recently converted, the developer must have initially funded a working
elements.label: text

elements.text: capital fund, through contributions made by the developer and/or purchasers of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Units, in an amount consistent with the estimated remaining life of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. There must be adequate funding for insurance deductible amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. At least 10% of the budget must provide funding for replacement reserves for capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenditures and deferred maintenance based on the project’s age, estimated remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: life and replacement cost of major Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculation of replacement reserve percentage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Formula Exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The replacement reserve percentage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined by dividing: (i) the annual
elements.label: text

elements.text: budgeted replacement reserve allocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: by (ii) the HOA’s annual budgeted
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment income (including regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: common expense fees)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The calculation may exclude:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Special assessment income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Income allocated to or in reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Incidental income not relied upon for
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance operations or capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. Amounts collected from unit owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: (but usually paid individually by them)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for items or utilities such as internet
elements.label: text

elements.text: access
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. If the budget does not provide a replacement reserve of at least 10%, a Seller may rely on
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. A reserve study, provided the conditions in Section 5701.6(k) below are met; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Contributions to a working capital fund, provided the conditions in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.6(l) below are met. These contributions can be in addition to or in lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any working capital fund contributions made by the developer in the case of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recently converted project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. An HOA must not receive more than 10% of its budgeted income from the rental or
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasing of commercial parking facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delinquent assessments for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) HOA assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of their HOA assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of each special assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Compliance with laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Condominium Project has been created and exists in full compliance with the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: State law, the requirements of the jurisdiction in which the Condominium Project is located,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and with all other applicable laws and regulations governing creation of the Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Limitations on ability to sell/right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any right of first refusal in the Project Documents will not adversely impact the rights of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee or its assignee to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclose or take title to a Condominium Unit pursuant to the remedies in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept a deed or assignment in lieu of foreclosure in the event of default by a mortgagor;
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell or lease a unit acquired by the mortgagee or its assignee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Conversions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Condominium Project that was created by conversion of a building(s) with a prior use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following requirements must be met for the Seller’s review and determination of project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. For a conversion involving a Non-Gut Rehabilitation of a prior use of the building that
elements.label: text

elements.text: was legally created within the past three years, the engineer’s report (or functionally
elements.label: text

elements.text: equivalent documentation for jurisdictions that do not require an engineer’s report) must
elements.label: text

elements.text: state:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the project is structurally sound
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The condition and remaining useful life of the major project components are
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient to meet the residential needs of the project; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That there is no evidence that any of these conditions have not been met
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Major components include the roof, elevators and mechanical systems such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: HVAC, plumbing and electricity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. All rehabilitation work involved in the conversion (Non-Gut Rehabilitation and Gut
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rehabilitation) must be completed in a professional manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A review of the engineer’s report (or functionally equivalent documentation) is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required for conversions involving:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Gut Rehabilitation, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Non-Gut Rehabilitation if more than three years have elapsed since the legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: creation of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Mortgagee consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Project Documents or applicable State law must provide that amendments of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: material adverse nature to First Lien mortgagees be agreed to by mortgagees that
elements.label: text

elements.text: represent at least 51% of the unit votes (based on one vote for each first Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned) subject to First Lien Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Project Documents or applicable State law must provide that any action to terminate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the legal status of the project or to use insurance proceeds for any purpose other than to
elements.label: text

elements.text: rebuild must be agreed to by First Lien mortgagees that represent at least 51% of the unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: votes (based on one vote for each first Mortgage owned) that are subject to First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Project Documents may allow implied approval to be assumed when the then current
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee of record fails to submit a response to any written proposal for an amendment
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 60 days after the then current mortgagee of record actually receives proper notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the proposal, provided the notice was delivered by certified or registered mail, with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “return receipt” requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Rights of Condominium mortgagees and guarantors
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Project Documents, applicable State law, or any applicable insurance policy must give
elements.label: text

elements.text: the mortgagee and guarantor of the Mortgage on any unit in a Condominium Project the right
elements.label: text

elements.text: to timely written notice of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Any condemnation or casualty loss that affects either a material portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project or the unit securing its Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Any 60-day Delinquency in the payment of assessments or charges owed by the owner of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any unit for which it holds the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A lapse, cancelation, or material reduction of any insurance policy maintained by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HOA
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Any proposed action that requires the consent of a specified percentage of mortgagees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) First mortgagee’s rights confirmed
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Project Documents must not give a Condominium Unit owner or any other party priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: over any rights of the first mortgagee of the Condominium Unit pursuant to its Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the case of payment to the unit owner of proceeds from termination, or insurance proceeds or
elements.label: text

elements.text: condemnation awards for losses to or a taking of Condominium Units and/or Common
elements.label: text

elements.text: Elements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Requirements when a Seller relies on a project reserve study for New Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The reserve study must comply with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The reserve study generally must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An inventory of major components of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financial analysis and evaluation of current reserve fund adequacy; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proposed annual reserve funding plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A reserve study’s financial analysis must validate that the project has appropriately
elements.label: text

elements.text: allocated the recommended reserve funds to provide the Condominium Project with
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient financial protection comparable to Freddie Mac’s standard budget requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for replacement reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The reserve study’s annual reserve funding plan, which details total costs identified for
elements.label: text

elements.text: replacement components, must meet or exceed the study’s recommendation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The most current reserve study (or update) must be dated within 36 months of the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination that a Condominium Project is eligible (see Section 5701.2(a)(2))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The reserve study must be prepared by an independent expert skilled in performing such
elements.label: text

elements.text: studies (such as a reserve study professional, a construction engineer, a certified public
elements.label: text

elements.text: accountant who specializes in reserve studies, or any professional with demonstrated
elements.label: text

elements.text: experience and knowledge in completing reserve studies)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The reserve study must meet or exceed requirements set forth in any applicable state
elements.label: text

elements.text: statutes
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. The reserve study must comment favorably on the project’s age, estimated remaining life,
elements.label: text

elements.text: structural integrity and the replacement of major components
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller relies on a reserve study that meets the requirements of this section, the project’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: budget must contain appropriate allocations to support the costs identified in the study.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain and retain in the Mortgage file a copy of the reserve study. The Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must also perform an analysis of the study and retain this analysis in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for the New Condominium Projects review type, the Condominium Project must
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet the definition of a New Condominium Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the project review and eligibility requirements in Section 5701.2, Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: secured by Condominium Units in New Condominium Projects must comply with all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Project completion requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The subject legal phase (or the subject building) and any prior legal phases in which units
elements.label: text

elements.text: have been offered for sale must be substantially complete. “Substantially complete” indicates
elements.label: text

elements.text: that the Common Elements are complete and the units are complete subject to the selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: of buyer preference items.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purpose of determining project completion under this Section 5701.6, a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: building can only have one legal phase, regardless of whether the Condominium Project is
elements.label: text

elements.text: comprised solely of that single building or multiple buildings. Legal phases are defined by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Project Documents. Construction or marketing phases developed for the convenience of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the developer are not necessarily legal phases and are not eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Owner-occupancy requirements for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: At least 50% of the total units in the project (or at least 50% of the sum of the subject legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: phase and prior legal phases) must have been conveyed or must be under contract to
elements.label: text

elements.text: purchasers who will occupy the units as their Primary Residences or second homes.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the purpose of calculating owner-occupancy under this Section 5701.6, a single building
elements.label: text

elements.text: can only have one legal phase, regardless of whether the Condominium Project is comprised
elements.label: text

elements.text: solely of that single building or multiple buildings. Legal phases are defined by the Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents. Construction or marketing phases developed for the convenience of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: developer are not necessarily legal phases and are not eligible.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Project budget requirement for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project’s budget for the current fiscal year must comply with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Homeowners Association’s (HOA’s) assessments must begin once the developer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: ceased to pay operating expenses attributable to the Condominium Project, whether or
elements.label: text

elements.text: not all units have been sold. When any unit owner other than the developer pays
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments, the developer must pay the assessments attributable to the unsold units.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The project’s budget (or its projected budget if the project has not been turned over to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit owners) must be consistent with the nature of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Appropriate assessments must be established to manage the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. There must be appropriate allocations for line items pertinent to the type and status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the project was recently converted, the developer must have initially funded a working
elements.label: text

elements.text: capital fund, through contributions made by the developer and/or purchasers of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Units, in an amount consistent with the estimated remaining life of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. There must be adequate funding for insurance deductible amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. At least 10% of the budget must provide funding for replacement reserves for capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenditures and deferred maintenance based on the project’s age, estimated remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: life and replacement cost of major Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculation of replacement reserve percentage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Formula Exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The replacement reserve percentage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined by dividing: (i) the annual
elements.label: text

elements.text: budgeted replacement reserve allocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: by (ii) the HOA’s annual budgeted
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment income (including regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: common expense fees)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The calculation may exclude:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Special assessment income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Income allocated to or in reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Incidental income not relied upon for
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance operations or capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. Amounts collected from unit owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: (but usually paid individually by them)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for items or utilities such as internet
elements.label: text

elements.text: access
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. If the budget does not provide a replacement reserve of at least 10%, a Seller may rely on
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. A reserve study, provided the conditions in Section 5701.6(k) below are met; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Contributions to a working capital fund, provided the conditions in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.6(l) below are met. These contributions can be in addition to or in lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any working capital fund contributions made by the developer in the case of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recently converted project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. An HOA must not receive more than 10% of its budgeted income from the rental or
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasing of commercial parking facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delinquent assessments for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) HOA assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of their HOA assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of each special assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Compliance with laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Condominium Project has been created and exists in full compliance with the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: State law, the requirements of the jurisdiction in which the Condominium Project is located,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and with all other applicable laws and regulations governing creation of the Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Limitations on ability to sell/right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any right of first refusal in the Project Documents will not adversely impact the rights of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee or its assignee to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclose or take title to a Condominium Unit pursuant to the remedies in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept a deed or assignment in lieu of foreclosure in the event of default by a mortgagor;
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell or lease a unit acquired by the mortgagee or its assignee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Conversions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Condominium Project that was created by conversion of a building(s) with a prior use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following requirements must be met for the Seller’s review and determination of project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. For a conversion involving a Non-Gut Rehabilitation of a prior use of the building that
elements.label: text

elements.text: was legally created within the past three years, the engineer’s report (or functionally
elements.label: text

elements.text: equivalent documentation for jurisdictions that do not require an engineer’s report) must
elements.label: text

elements.text: state:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the project is structurally sound
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The condition and remaining useful life of the major project components are
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient to meet the residential needs of the project; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That there is no evidence that any of these conditions have not been met
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Major components include the roof, elevators and mechanical systems such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: HVAC, plumbing and electricity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. All rehabilitation work involved in the conversion (Non-Gut Rehabilitation and Gut
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rehabilitation) must be completed in a professional manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A review of the engineer’s report (or functionally equivalent documentation) is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required for conversions involving:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Gut Rehabilitation, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Non-Gut Rehabilitation if more than three years have elapsed since the legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: creation of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Mortgagee consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Project Documents or applicable State law must provide that amendments of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: material adverse nature to First Lien mortgagees be agreed to by mortgagees that
elements.label: text

elements.text: represent at least 51% of the unit votes (based on one vote for each first Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned) subject to First Lien Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Project Documents or applicable State law must provide that any action to terminate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the legal status of the project or to use insurance proceeds for any purpose other than to
elements.label: text

elements.text: rebuild must be agreed to by First Lien mortgagees that represent at least 51% of the unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: votes (based on one vote for each first Mortgage owned) that are subject to First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Project Documents may allow implied approval to be assumed when the then current
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee of record fails to submit a response to any written proposal for an amendment
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 60 days after the then current mortgagee of record actually receives proper notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the proposal, provided the notice was delivered by certified or registered mail, with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “return receipt” requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: the mortgagee and guarantor of the Mortgage on any unit in a Condominium Project the right

elements.text: to timely written notice of:

elements.text: The project’s budget for the current fiscal year must comply with the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Homeowners Association’s (HOA’s) assessments must begin once the developer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: ceased to pay operating expenses attributable to the Condominium Project, whether or
elements.label: text

elements.text: not all units have been sold. When any unit owner other than the developer pays
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments, the developer must pay the assessments attributable to the unsold units.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The project’s budget (or its projected budget if the project has not been turned over to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: unit owners) must be consistent with the nature of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Appropriate assessments must be established to manage the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. There must be appropriate allocations for line items pertinent to the type and status of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If the project was recently converted, the developer must have initially funded a working
elements.label: text

elements.text: capital fund, through contributions made by the developer and/or purchasers of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Units, in an amount consistent with the estimated remaining life of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. There must be adequate funding for insurance deductible amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. At least 15% of the budget must provide funding for replacement reserves for capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenditures and deferred maintenance based on the project’s age, estimated remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: life and replacement cost of major Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Calculation of replacement reserve percentage
elements.label: text

elements.text: Formula Exclusions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The replacement reserve percentage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined by dividing: (i) the annual
elements.label: text

elements.text: budgeted replacement reserve allocation
elements.label: text

elements.text: by (ii) the HOA’s annual budgeted
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment income (including regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: common expense fees)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The calculation may exclude:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. Special assessment income
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Income allocated to or in reserve
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: iii. Incidental income not relied upon for
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance operations or capital
elements.label: text

elements.text: improvements; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: iv. Amounts collected from unit owners
elements.label: text

elements.text: (but usually paid individually by them)
elements.label: text

elements.text: for items or utilities such as internet
elements.label: text

elements.text: access
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. If the budget does not provide a replacement reserve of at least 15%, a Seller may rely on
elements.label: text

elements.text: either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: i. A reserve study, provided the conditions in Section 5701.6(k) below are met; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ii. Contributions to a working capital fund, provided the conditions in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.6(l) below are met. These contributions can be in addition to or in lieu of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any working capital fund contributions made by the developer in the case of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: recently converted project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. An HOA must not receive more than 10% of its budgeted income from the rental or
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasing of commercial parking facilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delinquent assessments for New Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) HOA assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of their HOA assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Special assessments
elements.label: text

elements.text: No more than 15% of the total number of units in a project are 60 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent in the payment of each special assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Compliance with laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Condominium Project has been created and exists in full compliance with the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: State law, the requirements of the jurisdiction in which the Condominium Project is located,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and with all other applicable laws and regulations governing creation of the Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Limitations on ability to sell/right of first refusal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any right of first refusal in the Project Documents will not adversely impact the rights of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee or its assignee to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Foreclose or take title to a Condominium Unit pursuant to the remedies in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accept a deed or assignment in lieu of foreclosure in the event of default by a mortgagor;
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sell or lease a unit acquired by the mortgagee or its assignee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Conversions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Condominium Project that was created by conversion of a building(s) with a prior use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following requirements must be met for the Seller’s review and determination of project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. For a conversion involving a Non-Gut Rehabilitation of a prior use of the building that
elements.label: text

elements.text: was legally created within the past three years, the engineer’s report (or functionally
elements.label: text

elements.text: equivalent documentation for jurisdictions that do not require an engineer’s report) must
elements.label: text

elements.text: state:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the project is structurally sound
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The condition and remaining useful life of the major project components are
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient to meet the residential needs of the project; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That there is no evidence that any of these conditions have not been met
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Major components include the roof, elevators and mechanical systems such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: HVAC, plumbing and electricity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. All rehabilitation work involved in the conversion (Non-Gut Rehabilitation and Gut
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rehabilitation) must be completed in a professional manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A review of the engineer’s report (or functionally equivalent documentation) is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: required for conversions involving:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Gut Rehabilitation, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Non-Gut Rehabilitation if more than three years have elapsed since the legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: creation of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Mortgagee consent
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Project Documents or applicable State law must provide that amendments of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: material adverse nature to First Lien mortgagees be agreed to by mortgagees that
elements.label: text

elements.text: represent at least 51% of the unit votes (based on one vote for each first Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: owned) subject to First Lien Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Project Documents or applicable State law must provide that any action to terminate
elements.label: text

elements.text: the legal status of the project or to use insurance proceeds for any purpose other than to
elements.label: text

elements.text: rebuild must be agreed to by First Lien mortgagees that represent at least 51% of the unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: votes (based on one vote for each first Mortgage owned) that are subject to First Lien
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Project Documents may allow implied approval to be assumed when the then current
elements.label: text

elements.text: mortgagee of record fails to submit a response to any written proposal for an amendment
elements.label: text

elements.text: within 60 days after the then current mortgagee of record actually receives proper notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the proposal, provided the notice was delivered by certified or registered mail, with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: “return receipt” requested
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Rights of Condominium mortgagees and guarantors
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Project Documents, applicable State law, or any applicable insurance policy must give
elements.label: text

elements.text: the mortgagee and guarantor of the Mortgage on any unit in a Condominium Project the right
elements.label: text

elements.text: to timely written notice of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Any condemnation or casualty loss that affects either a material portion of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Project or the unit securing its Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Any 60-day Delinquency in the payment of assessments or charges owed by the owner of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any unit for which it holds the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. A lapse, cancelation, or material reduction of any insurance policy maintained by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: HOA
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Any proposed action that requires the consent of a specified percentage of mortgagees
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) First mortgagee’s rights confirmed
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Project Documents must not give a Condominium Unit owner or any other party priority
elements.label: text

elements.text: over any rights of the first mortgagee of the Condominium Unit pursuant to its Mortgage in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the case of payment to the unit owner of proceeds from termination, or insurance proceeds or
elements.label: text

elements.text: condemnation awards for losses to or a taking of Condominium Units and/or Common
elements.label: text

elements.text: Elements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (k) Requirements when a Seller relies on a project reserve study for New Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: The reserve study must comply with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The reserve study generally must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An inventory of major components of the project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Financial analysis and evaluation of current reserve fund adequacy; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Proposed annual reserve funding plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A reserve study’s financial analysis must validate that the project has appropriately
elements.label: text

elements.text: allocated the recommended reserve funds to provide the Condominium Project with
elements.label: text

elements.text: sufficient financial protection comparable to Freddie Mac’s standard budget requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: for replacement reserves
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The reserve study’s annual reserve funding plan, which details total costs identified for
elements.label: text

elements.text: replacement components, must meet or exceed the study’s recommendation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conclusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The most current reserve study (or update) must be dated within 36 months of the Seller’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination that a Condominium Project is eligible (see Section 5701.2(a)(2))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The reserve study must be prepared by an independent expert skilled in performing such
elements.label: text

elements.text: studies (such as a reserve study professional, a construction engineer, a certified public
elements.label: text

elements.text: accountant who specializes in reserve studies, or any professional with demonstrated
elements.label: text

elements.text: experience and knowledge in completing reserve studies)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The reserve study must meet or exceed requirements set forth in any applicable state
elements.label: text

elements.text: statutes
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. The reserve study must comment favorably on the project’s age, estimated remaining life,
elements.label: text

elements.text: structural integrity and the replacement of major components
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller relies on a reserve study that meets the requirements of this section, the project’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: budget must contain the highest recommended reserve allocation amount in the study to
elements.label: text

elements.text: support the costs identified in the study. Reserve studies that establish a reserve funding goal
elements.label: text

elements.text: that allows the reserve cash balance to approach but never fall below zero during the cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: flow projection, referred to as baseline funding method, must not be used to waive the 15%
elements.label: text

elements.text: reserve requirement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller must obtain and retain in the Mortgage file a copy of the reserve study. The Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: must also perform an analysis of the study and retain this analysis in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (l) Requirements when a Seller relies on contributions to a working capital fund for New
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Seller relies on contributions to a working capital fund, the following requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Project Documents must require the purchaser of a Condominium Unit to pay a non￾refundable and non-transferable assessment to a working capital fund which must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: established for the periodic maintenance, repair and replacement of the Common
elements.label: text

elements.text: Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The assessment must be equal to a minimum of at least two months of the HOA fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: attributable to the Condominium Unit and be due and payable at closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The developer is in control of the HOA
elements.label: text

elements.text: (m)New Condominium Project sold with excessive Seller contributions
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a builder, developer or property seller offers financing or sale arrangements (such as rent￾backs, payments of principal, interest, taxes and insurance) for Condominium Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages in a New Condominium Project these contributions must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interested party contribution requirements of Section 5501.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the requirements for New Condominium Projects in this Section 5701.6 are met, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller is not required to comply with the requirements for any of the other project review
elements.label: text

elements.text: types in Sections 5701.5 (Established Condominium Project reviews), 5701.7 (Exempt from
elements.label: text

elements.text: review) and 5701.9 (Reciprocal project reviews).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.7: Exempt From Review (05/06/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condominium Project eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible under Exempt From Review, the requirements below must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Eligible Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage must be one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by a Condominium Unit in a 2- to 4-Unit Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by a Condominium Unit in a 5- to 10-Unit Condominium Project that is not part
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a Master Association
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secured by a Detached Condominium Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance Condominium Unit Mortgage; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Refi Possible® Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Condominium Project eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Condominium Project must meet the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be a Condominium Hotel or similar type of transient housing, a houseboat project, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: timeshare project, or a project with segmented ownership (all as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not include Manufactured Homes, unless the Condominium Unit Mortgage is a Refi
elements.label: text

elements.text: Possible Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance Condominium Unit Mortgages and Refi
elements.label: text

elements.text: Possible Mortgages, the Condominium Project is not in need of Critical Repairs and does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not have an evacuation order. (See Sections 5701.3(n) and 5701.3(o) for details.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be the subject of any action that would cause the Condominium Project to cease to
elements.label: text

elements.text: exist and not be the subject of an insolvency proceeding. (See Section 5701.3(p) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: details.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) General project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The general project eligibility requirements in Section 5701.2(b) must be met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, attached and semi detached units within projects containing Detached
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Units may not be delivered as Exempt From Review unless:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ They are within a 2- to 4-Unit Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ They are within a 5- to 10-Unit Condominium Project that is not part of a Master
elements.label: text

elements.text: Association; or

elements.text: ■ They meet the requirements for Freddie Mac-owned “no cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Appraisal requirements for Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Appraisal requirements for Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General appraisal requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must report:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The project name
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The assessments, including special assessments; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The property rights for each comparable sale and compare them to the subject project
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser must also identify the Common Elements including the Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: available to the unit owners, comment on their condition and analyze how they compare
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Common Elements and Amenities of competing projects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Comparable sales must be from similar Condominium Projects in the same market and
elements.label: text

elements.text: compete for the same purchasers.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Comparable sales selection
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 5605.6(g) for specific requirements applicable to units in Established
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects or in recently converted or New Condominium Projects.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Additional appraisal requirements for Detached Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser should use similar Detached Condominium Unit comparable sales from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: same project or from the same Market Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The appraiser may use other types of 1-unit detached comparable sales that are not
elements.label: text

elements.text: located in a Condominium Project if the appraiser supports the use of such sales in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appraisal report and reflects any effect that the condominium form of ownership has on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the market value and marketability of the subject property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each appraisal report must comply with the appraisal requirements in Sections 5604.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 5604.2 and Chapter 5605.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Underwriting considerations for Condominium Projects with mixed uses and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects with live-work Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Condominium Projects with mixed uses
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase eligible Condominium Mortgages in Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a combination of residential, commercial, industrial, office and/or institutional uses
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that the Condominium Mortgages comply with all applicable Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements, including the Condominium Project review and general Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project eligibility requirements of Section 5701.2 and the project with excessive
elements.label: text

elements.text: commercial or non-residential space requirements in Section 5701.3(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Live-work Condominium Units
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase eligible Condominium Mortgages in Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: with live-work Condominium Units provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Condominium Mortgage complies with all applicable Freddie Mac requirements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the Condominium Project review and general Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility requirements of Section 5701.2; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The primary use of the live-work Condominium Unit is residential and the non￾residential use of such Condominium Unit is secondary
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Underwriting considerations for Common Elements and Amenities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The project’s Common Elements, including Amenities and Limited Common Elements, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: be consistent with the nature of the project and similar to those found in competing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects within the Market Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Financing of Limited Common Elements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Limited Common Elements are portions of Common Elements reserved for use by one or
elements.label: text

elements.text: more unit owners but not all unit owners. They are defined in the Project Documents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: may include, but are not limited to, balconies or patios serving a single unit, assigned parking
elements.label: text

elements.text: spaces or storage bins.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Limited Common Elements that are purchased as part of the Condominium Unit may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: financed as part of the Mortgage, and the cost of such Limited Common Elements may be
elements.label: text

elements.text: included when determining the sale price and loan-to-value (LTV) ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Only Limited Common Elements may be financed along with the Condominium Unit.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Facilities serving the Condominium Unit which are made available to the Condominium Unit
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a permit, license or lease (other than in a leasehold condominium), must not be financed
elements.label: text

elements.text: as part of a Mortgage, and the cost of the use of such facilities may not be included when
elements.label: text

elements.text: determining the sale price and LTV ratio.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.9: Reciprocal project reviews (05/06/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fannie Mae-approved and certified projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ FHA-Approved Project review for condominiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit Mortgages secured by Condominium Units located in Condominium Projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by other secondary market participants are eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit Mortgages comply with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Fannie Mae-approved and certified projects
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Mortgages secured by 1-unit residential dwellings in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Projects that either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have an “Approved by Fannie Mae” status designation in Fannie Mae’s Condo Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ManagerTM (CPMTM); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller approved as a Fannie Mae “Full Review,” ensured all data entered into CPM
elements.label: text

elements.text: were correct and received a “Certified by Lender” status designation in Fannie Mae’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: legacy or updated CPM platform
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Condo Project Manager and CPM are trademarks of Fannie Mae.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Requirements as of the Settlement Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: As of the Settlement Date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The project complies with all applicable Fannie Mae eligibility requirements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: lender warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The approval or certification and any terms and conditions set forth in the approval or
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification have not expired
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The Mortgage complies with any stated CPM delivery restrictions (e.g., occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: type restriction, maximum loan-to-value (LTV) ratios, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Seller complies with Fannie Mae’s additional obligations for projects approved or
elements.label: text

elements.text: certified through CPM. Pursuant to these obligations, if the Seller notified Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae’s CPM Management team of any new information that may impact a project’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility (e.g., significant deferred maintenance, litigation, etc.), prior to selling the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage to Freddie Mac, the Seller must confirm that the project retained its
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved status.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Mortgage file contains documentation of Fannie Mae’s approval as evidenced by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An “Approved by Fannie Mae” status designation (e.g., a copy of the appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPM screen showing that the Condominium Project has an “Approved by Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae” status designation); or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation of the Seller’s project approval as a Fannie Mae “Full Review”
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a “Certified by Lender” status designation from Fannie Mae’s legacy CPM
elements.label: text

elements.text: (certification exported from Fannie Mae Connect) or Fannie Mae’s updated CPM
elements.label: text

elements.text: platform (certification printed from the updated CPM platform)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The Condominium Project complies with the general project eligibility requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 5701.2(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: As required in Section 5701.2(b)(6), the Seller must retain, and provide upon request,
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation to support its analysis that the Condominium Project meets Fannie Mae’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Full Review” requirements when the Seller received a “Certified by Lender” status
elements.label: text

elements.text: designation in Fannie Mae’s legacy or updated CPM platform.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Condominium Projects comprised of Manufactured Homes
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages secured by Condominium Units in Condominium Projects comprised of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manufactured Homes are only eligible for sale to Freddie Mac if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The project has an “Approved by Fannie Mae” status designation in CPM; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When they comply with the requirements in Section 5701.5(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) FHA-Approved Project review for condominiums
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will purchase Condominium Unit Mortgages secured by 1-unit residential
elements.label: text

elements.text: dwellings in Condominium Projects that have an FHA-Approved Project review if all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following requirements are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Approval process and requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Condominium Project must appear on the list of projects approved by the FHA,
elements.label: text

elements.text: either by the FHA HUD Review and Approval Process (HRAP) or the FHA Direct
elements.label: text

elements.text: Endorsement Lender Review and Approval Process (DELRAP), provided that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is an FHA Mortgage, VA Mortgage, Section 502 GRH Mortgage, or HUD￾Guaranteed Section 184 Native American Mortgage that complies with the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Guide, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The Condominium Project must appear on the list of projects approved by FHA
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the FHA HRAP, provided that the Mortgage is a conventional Mortgage and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Condominium Project meets the definition of an Established Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Condominium Project is not an ineligible project (Section 5701.3)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All general project eligibility requirements in Section 5701.2(b) must be met, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage must comply with all other applicable requirements of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Seller warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller sells Freddie Mac a Mortgage secured by a unit in a project that meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements for FHA-Approved Projects above, the Seller warrants all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following as of the Settlement Date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The project is in the “approved” status
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The project complies with any FHA-approval conditions noted on the FHA website
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The approval has not yet expired and has not been rescinded or modified in any way
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Mortgage file contains documentation of FHA’s approved status (for example, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the appropriate webpage showing that the project is approved and that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval is current)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Seller is not aware of any circumstances that would make the project ineligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the requirements for reciprocal project reviews in this Section 5701.9 are met, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller is not required to comply with the requirements for any of the other project review types in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 5701.4 (Streamlined reviews), 5701.5 (Established Condominium Project reviews),
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.6 (New Condominium Project reviews) and 5701.7 (Exempt from review).

elements.text: When the Seller sells Freddie Mac a Mortgage secured by a unit in a project that meets
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements for FHA-Approved Projects above, the Seller warrants all of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following as of the Settlement Date:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The project is in the “approved” status
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The project complies with any FHA-approval conditions noted on the FHA website
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The approval has not yet expired and has not been rescinded or modified in any way
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The Mortgage file contains documentation of FHA’s approved status (for example, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the appropriate webpage showing that the project is approved and that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval is current)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. The Seller is not aware of any circumstances that would make the project ineligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the requirements for reciprocal project reviews in this Section 5701.9 are met, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller is not required to comply with the requirements for any of the other project review types in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 5701.5 (Established Condominium Project reviews), 5701.6 (New Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project reviews) and 5701.7 (Exempt from review).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.10: Condo Project Advisor® – Project
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Assessment Request (PAR) (05/06/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condo Project Advisor® (CPASM) allows authorized Sellers to submit a Project Assessment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Request (PAR) to obtain feedback (referred to as the PAR findings) regarding a Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project’s compliance with the project review requirements assessed by CPA. A Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project’s PAR findings are also available in Loan Product Advisor® when CPA identifies the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property address submitted in Loan Product Advisor and the project has a PAR status assigned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgages eligible for purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Submission timing and Seller Certified status expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage identification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assessed and non-assessed project review requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Project Certified Submission (PCS) review process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller Review Certification (SRC) process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Documentation requirements for Mortgages with PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Mortgages eligible for purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: To be eligible for purchase the Mortgage must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be secured by a Condominium Unit in an Established Condominium Project
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be a Loan Product Advisor Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not be secured by a Condominium Unit in a Condominium Project with a Not Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: status PAR finding
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Submission timing and Seller Certified status expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Submission timing
elements.label: text

elements.text: For CPA, the PAR and, if applicable, the required data for a Seller Review Certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: (SRC), may be submitted at any time during the loan origination process but must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted prior to sale of the related Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Loan Product Advisor, the Mortgage must be submitted to Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: pursuant to the requirements in Section 5101.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Seller Certified status expiration
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller Certified status PAR finding expires one year from the SRC submission date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The expiration date is specified on the Condo Project Advisor Feedback Certificate or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Last Feedback Certificate, whichever contains the last PAR findings as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5701.10(d)(iii). If the Seller Certified status PAR finding expires before the Note Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Seller wants to rely on the Seller Certified status, Sellers must submit a new SRC in
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA prior to sale of the related Mortgage to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Mortgage identification
elements.label: text

elements.text: The PAR is applicable only to the Mortgage identified on the Condo Project Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Feedback Certificate or the Last Feedback Certificate, as applicable, and is not applicable to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other Mortgage secured by a Condominium Unit located in the same Condominium
elements.label: text

elements.text: Project.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage identified will be the Mortgage for which the Seller enters one of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following in CPA:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The LP Key Number, an alphanumeric identifier, listed on the Feedback Certificate,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which may also be referred to as the LP AUS Key Number or the Loan Prospector Key
elements.label: text

elements.text: Number, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller loan number/identifier
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Retail Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Retail Mortgages, the LP Key Number or the Seller loan number must be entered into
elements.label: text

elements.text: CPA, and it must be identical to the LP Key Number or Seller loan number delivered in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Selling Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Non-Retail Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: For non-Retail Mortgages, the LP Key Number must be entered into CPA, and it must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: identical to the LP Key Number delivered in Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) PAR findings
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller is responsible for ensuring compliance with the project review requirements specified
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the last PAR findings (see Section 5701.10(d)(iii) below). See Section 5701.10(i) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: details about representations and warranties.
elements.label: text
